target.title: config validate rejects plugin-owned channel schema extensions
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw@2026.6.6 config validate` appears to reject plugin-owned channel config extensions before installed plugin metadata can extend the bundled channel schema.

This blocks a guarded replacement for the Slack extension that adds a schema-backed `channels.slack.threadGuard` object while keeping compatibility with OpenClaw `2026.6.6`.

## Environment

- OpenClaw CLI/package: `openclaw@2026.6.6`
- Upstream Slack extension basis: `openclaw/openclaw` `v2026.6.6`, `extensions/slack`
- External plugin bundle installs a replacement Slack extension into the OpenClaw extensions directory.

## Observed behavior

With the replacement Slack plugin installed in a temporary OpenClaw home, `openclaw@2026.6.6 config schema` exposes the plugin-provided schema including:

```text
channels.slack.properties.threadGuard
```

But validating a config containing the same `channels.slack.threadGuard` object fails:

```text
channels.slack: invalid config: must not have additional properties: "threadGuard"
```

This suggests `config validate` is doing an earlier/static raw validation of `channels.slack` before plugin metadata (`openclaw.plugin.json#channelConfigs.slack.schema`) is allowed to extend the channel config shape.

## Expected behavior

If an installed plugin contributes a channel config schema for an existing channel key, config validation should validate against the plugin-extended schema (or provide a documented validation mode for replacement plugins) instead of rejecting plugin-owned properties as additional properties.

## Why this matters

A production deployment wants to consume a guarded Slack plugin as a bundle copied into `/data/extensions/slack`, not through the operator `spec.plugins` installer. The guarded plugin adds schema-backed policy under `channels.slack.threadGuard` so validation can enforce thread-only/channel-only Slack operation.

The deployment is intentionally gated until `config validate` can validate the plugin-extended config shape or there is a documented supported validation path.

No secrets, tokens, or private config values are included here.
target.author: srausser
